steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The blank fills the word record, completing the noun phrase fossil record. Once that word is in place, the left side becomes a complete independent clause: they found something never before seen in the fossil record. What follows the colon is an appositive — evidence of cloning — that renames or elaborates on what they found. After the period begins a new independent clause starting with In clonal fragmentation.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
A colon introduces an elaboration after a complete clause; a period separates independent clauses. Choice D applies both correctly: the colon after record introduces the appositive evidence of cloning, and the period launches the new clause In clonal fragmentation…

  • Choice A: places a period after record and a colon later, cutting off the appositive from the clause it elaborates. ✗
  • Choice B: uses a comma after record, which is too weak to separate the following appositive from an independent clause that follows. ✗
  • Choice C: uses a colon after record but then places another colon before In clonal fragmentation, creating a double-colon structure that is not standard English punctuation. ✗
  • Choice D: colon after record + period before the new clause — both marks are correctly placed. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
